name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 77. **AWRP ~1.0% H&M / 7-DAY (some transits 0.8% post-NCB; 8x pre-war 0.10-0.15%; off March-peak 2.5%); 88% LMA hull underwriters still have appetite; 90% cargo appetite (S&P Global / Strauss Center)**. Some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED VIA BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 17 AT WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** (CONFIRMED — Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report independent operational primary; Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week now corroborated). ~4.2x late-January reading (Week 4 TD3C WS126.5 / $112,394/day) and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. **Week 18 partial shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade')** — still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added on brent-crude is now BASIS CONFIRMED ($467K is far above $200K threshold); 5-session sustain pending daily-print verification.** Hormuz 'CRITICAL' — 5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical. **C1 PARADOX: Brent refusing to fall to $105.68 while VLCC TD3C at $420K = paper-physical bifurcation deepest of cycle.** **C3 FRONTLINE -3.5% 1D / +8.9% 1M / +21.7% 3M — Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply against SPOT VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at $420K+/day**. Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery — investor should treat Frontline equity as leading indicator of de-escalation if it stays sold; treat VLCC TD3C as leading indicator of physical disruption if it stays elevated.** Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. **Container freight (FBX13) China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w) — Mediterranean +15% w/w consistent with Red Sea + Hormuz rerouting persisting.** Tanker-container divergence persists. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M). PRIOR (May 13): **AWRP UNCHANGED AT ~1% H&M / 7-DAY (8x PRE-WAR 0.1-0.15% baseline; off March-peak 2.5% but elevated); 88% LMA hull underwriters still have appetite (Lloyd's List May 6 update).** Trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 — FAILING HARD. **PHYSICAL REALITY REINFORCED — Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** = industry-CEO authority. **VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn at Gulf of Oman without crossing US blockade line = confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs.** Houthi Day 80 Red Sea quiet possibly broken May 11 (UKMTO advisory near Hodeidah; no claim) — if second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days, additional AWRP step-up likely. Second commercial vessel strike inside Qatari EEZ trigger (May 10 + 7 days) Day 2 of 7 NOT TRIPPED. Vessel attribution clarified: Safesea Neha (Marshall Islands, US-managed Safesea Group NJ). Container freight: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity has caught up; tanker market diverging from container market. VLCC TD3C anchored ~$100K/day; Baltic VLCC US Gulf-China $91,731/day; Frontline secured $76,900/day starting 2026 contract; spot premium intact. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE per markets dossier E4: marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude (CONFIRMED 1st-order edge; AWRP ~1% H&M = 8x pre-war is direct mechanism in Brent floor). Edge to be created with initial weight 5-6.** Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. The paper-vs-physical bifurcation continues: Frontline -3.4% 1D on Evercore ISI downgrade (2027-28 oversupply) vs Hormuz 2-5 ships/day physical tightening.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.

active_triggers.condition: Lloyd's JWC removes Arabian Gulf from conflict zone designation
AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions (by 2026-05-16) = pause priced as durable de-escalation
active_triggers.reframed_from: AWRP 5%+ within 5 sessions (Project Freedom effectiveness verification) — obsoleted by May 5 pause

recent_signals.headline: **MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL: Lloyd's List May 11 reports VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at ~$420-462K/day** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K from markets dossier May 13). **Paper-Brent at $105.68 vs spot VLCC at $420K = paper-physical bifurcation deepest of cycle.** Per markets dossier E proposal: proposed new edge VLCC-TD3C → Brent-Crude at initial weight 9.0; **figure flagged for cross-verification with Baltic Exchange before propagating.** **AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; off March-peak 2.5%); 88% LMA hull appetite, 90% cargo (S&P Global / Strauss Center).** Some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **C3 FRONTLINE -3.5% 1D / +8.9% 1M — Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply** against SPOT VLCC TD3C $420K+/day. Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. Frontline equity SELLING vs tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery. **NEW TRIGGER (added on brent-crude node): VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical divergence terminal escalation.** **Container freight (FBX13): China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w)** — Med rerouting persisting. JWC Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M).
sources: Lloyd's List May 11 (VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' $420-462K/day) — primary
S&P Global / Strauss Center (AWRP ~1%; LMA appetite 88% hull, 90% cargo)
recent_signals.verification: VLCC TD3C $420-462K/day = REPORTED (Lloyd's List primary — operationally large figure flagged for cross-verification); AWRP ~1% = CONFIRMED (S&P Global / Strauss Center primary); FBX13 = CONFIRMED (Freightos primary); Frontline -3.5% = CONFIRMED tape (yfinance); Frontline equity-vs-tanker-day-rate bifurcation = CONFIRMED analytical observation.

recent_signals.headline: AWRP UNCHANGED ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-DAY BASELINE; SUNDAY BULK CARRIER STRUCK 23NM NE OF DOHA = FRESH OPERATIONAL DATA POINT PUSHING AWRP UP NOT DOWN FOR MONDAY MAY 11 REPRICING. First commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM San Antonio May 5; FIRST inside Qatari EEZ; UKMTO Warning notice posted; perpetrator UNKNOWN. Trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. CENTCOM precision tally May 9: 58 vessels redirected + 4 Iranian-flagged tankers disabled since April 13 — 4x acceleration in kinetic enforcement vs prior 24 days. Lloyd's List ~6 vessels/day Hormuz transit vs ~60 baseline; ~1,500 vessels anchored. War risk surcharge differential: US/UK/Israeli vessels charged 3x non-aligned (Lloyd's List). Insurance market is the cleanest single signal of physical-tightness divorced from paper Brent price.
sources: UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
recent_signals.verification: Bulk carrier near Doha = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary, multi-source); perpetrator UNKNOWN; AWRP unchanged = CONFIRMED multi-source baseline; CENTCOM 58/4 tally = CONFIRMED ACTION

recent_signals.headline: AWRP AT ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-DAY BASELINE — INSURERS NOT YET PRICING MOU FRAMEWORK AS DURABLE; DAY 1-2 OF 'AWRP NORMALIZATION TOWARD 0.5% WITHIN 10 SESSIONS' TRIGGER (BY MAY 16). VLCC TD3C ~$420-474K/day TCE sustained; STEEPEST 1-DAY DROP IN 5+ YEARS ON MOU NEWS ('LEAD BALLOON' — Lloyd's List). Hormuz transits 5-6/day vs 138 norm. CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile keeps kinetic-tail risk live (CBS attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed). Paper-vs-physical bifurcation IS the structural intelligence.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED operational data multi-source; CMA CGM strike = CONFIRMED action, CLAIMED Iran attribution
